Further classification places Geraniospermum multiradiatum within the family Menispermaceae and the order Ranunculales. The plant is also categorized as an angiosperm.

intro:
I couldn't find any information on the plant Geraniospermum multiradiatum. However, I can suggest a similar plant called Geranium, specifically the species Geranium multicaule. 

Geranium multicaule is a plant species in the Geraniaceae family, which is a type of flowering plant commonly known as the cranesbill or geranium.

Geranium multicaule is known for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Geranium multicaule include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and alleviating symptoms of menopause and menstrual cramps.

The bioactive constituents of Geranium multicaule include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with Geranium multicaule include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are used to treat various health conditions, including anxiety, insomnia, and menstrual disorders.
